SALEvent.Description: Published once on each guider start/stop/pause/resume/stamp

item.Description: Enumeration with the current guiding status. When guider is not running status will be GuiderStatus_Stopped, this could either be when the guider is first enabled or after a stopGuiding command. When guider is initializing a guider sequence but has not achieved stable guiding operation status will be GuiderStatus_Starting. When stable guiding operation is stabilished, status will be GuiderStatus_Guiding. If an error occurred, and the guider is going to FAULT state, status is GuiderStatus_Error. Information about errors will be available in errorCode topic.
DAQ provided sequence number
DAQ provided stamp number
DAQ provided timestamp (TAI)
OBSID corresponding to this stamp (derived from DAQ comment field)
Sensors for which stamps were received (e.g. R00_SG1)

SALEvent.Description: Published once on each start of the guider (corresponding to initGuiders command)

item.Description: The number of rows
The number of columns
Integration time
Sensor, e.g. R00_SG0 (: delimited list)
The segment number within the sensor
ROI start row
ROI start column
True if the ROI is split across segments

SALEvent.Description: Event published when a guiding operatation starts, stops or resumes. It contains the parameters used to configure the guider (see startGuiding command) and the current status.

item.Description: Vector with 8 elements specifying the origin of the guider ROI window in the x-axis for each guider sensor.

Vector with 8 elements specifying the end of the guider ROI window in the x-axis for each guider sensor. If roiXLeft=roiXRight=0, for a particular element of both arrays, it assumes no guider region for the equivalent guider sensor. For each valid entry in roiXRight/roiXLeft the width must respect the ROI constrains (see requirement CA-TS-GDR-ICD-0006 in LSE-66).
Vector with 8 elements specifying the origin of the guider ROI window in the y-axis for each guider sensor.

Vector with 8 elements specifying the end of the guider ROI window in the y-axis for each guider sensor. If roiXBottom=roiXTop=0, for a particular element of both arrays, it assumes no guider region for the equivalent guider sensor. For each valid entry in roiXBottom/roiXTop the width must respect the ROI constrains (see requirement CA-TS-GDR-ICD-0006 in LSE-66).

Exposure time in milliseconds. All guider sensors will use the same exposure time (CA-TS-GDR-ICD-0034 LSE-66). Limits must respect detector integration time constrains (see requirement CA-TS-GDR-ICD-0034 in LSE-66).
Binnig factor. All guider sensors will use the same binning factor. Limits must respect detector bining constrains (see requirement CA-TS-GDR-ICD-0042 in LSE-66).

Enumeration with the current guiding status. When guider is not running status will be GuiderStatus_Stopped, this could either be when the guider is first enabled or after a stopGuiding command. When guider is initializing a guider sequence but has not achieved stable guiding operation status will be GuiderStatus_Starting. When stable guiding operation is stabilished, status will be GuiderStatus_Guiding. If an error occurred, and the guider is going to FAULT state, status is GuiderStatus_Error. Information about errors will be available in errorCode topic.
